questions.question: Why should solo founders prioritize their personal profile over their company page for lead generation?

options.text: Because personal profiles are cheaper to maintain.
Because they typically get 5-10x more reach and higher engagement through social interaction.
Because LinkedIn will delete company pages that don't have enough followers.
Because company pages are only for people who have employees.

questions.explanation: LinkedIn's algorithm and users both favor human interaction. Personal profiles are the primary 'Engine' for starting conversations and building trust.

questions.question: What is the primary 'Legitimacy' function of a company page for a solo founder?

options.text: To make you look like you have 100 employees.
To allow you to link your current role to an organization and display a logo in your Experience section.
To hide your identity from competitors.
To automatically post to other social networks.

questions.explanation: While growth happens on the personal profile, the company page provides the structural 'Anchor' that gives your business a professional presence on the platform.

questions.question: In the 'Hub and Spoke' strategy, which entity acts as the Hub?

options.text: The Company Page.
The Personal Profile.
The LinkedIn Newsletter.
Your website.

questions.explanation: The Personal Profile is the center of interaction (the Hub) where relationships are built and authority is demonstrated.

questions.question: What is 'Borg Talk' in the context of founder content?

options.text: A language used by AI bots.
A generic corporate tone using 'We' and 'Professional' platitudes that lack human personality.
A specific type of high-tech coding language.
The official terminology used by LinkedIn's support team.

questions.explanation: Borg Talk is the 'faceless' corporate language that founders often use to try and sound 'Big,' which actually destroys the trust and authenticity that a solo practitioner can offer.

questions.question: What is the key takeaway regarding 'Entity Authority' for a solo founder?

options.text: Entity Authority is found only in patents and trademarks.
Entity Authority resides in the person (the practitioner) who demonstrates real-world expertise.
Entity Authority is granted by LinkedIn when you get 5,000 followers.
Only company pages can have Entity Authority.

questions.explanation: For a solo founder, you ARE the entity. Your personal track record and practitioner voice are what the AI and human users trust.

questions.question: Go to your 'Experience' section on your LinkedIn profile. Is your business entry linked to a page with a logo, or is it a gray placeholder box? If you don't have a logo linked, how will this impact a prospect's initial perception of your 'Legitimacy' arm of the Trust Pincer?
promptHints: Think about the difference between a 'Project' and a 'Business'.
Consider how a gray box looks compared to a clean, branded logo entry.
Reflect on the 'Professional Identity' function mentioned in the lesson.

title: Personal Brand vs. Company Page - Quiz
